description_long: This object is a realistic crab with a broad, slightly convex carapace that is mottled in shades of tan, beige, and gray. Its two large claws (chelae) are prominently displayed at the front, with the right claw appearing slightly larger than the left. The crab has eight slender walking legs, each jointed and tapering to a point, splayed outward from the body. Two long, thin antennae extend upward from the front of the carapace, and its eyes are set on short stalks. The surface of the crab is hard and slightly rough, with a matte finish typical of a dried or preserved exoskeleton. The overall appearance suggests a naturalistic representation suitable for educational or decorative use in an urban setting.
description: A tan and gray crab with large claws, eight jointed legs, and prominent antennae, featuring a hard, matte exoskeleton. The eyes are set on short stalks at the front of the carapace.
description_view_0: Front view showing both large claws, antennae, and eyes on stalks, with the carapace and all legs visible.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the right claw, the curvature of the carapace, and the segmentation of the legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the carapace, the attachment points of the legs, and the symmetry of the body.
description_view_3: Angled side view from the left, emphasizing the left claw, the side profile of the carapace, and the arrangement of the legs.
